brand.name: Open Finance
brand.tagline: Private money tracking, without the spreadsheet sprawl
brand.description: Open Finance helps people track subscriptions, budgets, spending, and finance records in one private workspace. It is a local-first finance tracker: users can manage money data on their device, import statements, review dashboards, sync with Google Sheets if they want, and optionally back up to Cloudflare.

hero.label: Bangkok / local-first finance
headline: Private money tracking, without the
hero.lead: Subscriptions, budgets, statements, spending records, and finance dashboards live in one private workspace on the user's device.

headline: Finance records should feel
, not outsourced.
about.body: Open Finance is for people who want the clarity of a finance dashboard without handing every transaction to another cloud-first app. The product keeps the workspace local by default, then lets the user decide when to connect Google Sheets or Cloudflare backup.

cards.title: Recurring spend radar
cards.body: Track active plans, renewal dates, category drift, and cancellation candidates in a compact ledger.

cards.title: Envelope-style planning
cards.body: Budget by month, category, and personal rule without turning the interface into a spreadsheet clone.

cards.title: Import, clean, review
cards.body: Bring in bank exports, map columns, flag duplicates, and keep source files visible for audit.

cards.title: A durable finance archive
cards.body: Keep notes, receipts, tax-relevant records, and spending context close to the transactions they explain.

cards.title: Subscription Watch
cards.body: Renewal timeline, price-change notes, and cancel-by reminders.

cards.title: Budget Board
cards.body: Monthly envelopes with actuals, remaining room, and rollover notes.

cards.title: Statement Import
cards.body: CSV mapping, duplicate review, and confidence labels before commit.

cards.title: Sheet Mirror
cards.body: Push selected views to Google Sheets when collaboration beats privacy.

cards.title: Cloudflare Vault
cards.body: Encrypted off-device copy when the user chooses resilience.

steps.title: Keep it local
steps.body: Start with device-first storage and an explicit workspace boundary.

steps.title: Normalize imports
steps.body: Turn messy statement exports into reviewable rows before they enter the ledger.

steps.title: Show the money map
steps.body: Surface subscriptions, budgets, spending, and records as one connected system.

steps.title: Sync only by choice
steps.body: Google Sheets and Cloudflare backup remain optional, visible, and reversible.

cards.title: Ledger view
cards.body: A dense transaction table with filters, category chips, and duplicate states.

cards.title: Budget studio
cards.body: A monthly planning surface that balances quiet editorial hierarchy with product clarity.

testimonial.quote: Private finance software should make the local copy feel like the source of truth.
testimonial.author: Open Finance product note

headline: Start with the
cta.lead: Open the preview, import a statement, and decide later whether any part of the workspace deserves to sync.
